_comment: Example .autoresearch.json. Copy to your vault as .autoresearch.json and edit. This file is strict JSON (no // comments); documentation lives in '_comment' keys, which the loader ignores. Every block below is OPTIONAL: omit a block to accept the documented default in scripts/vault_config.py. See CLAUDE.method.md.
_comment_subject: Legacy single-subject label. If 'anchor.people' is empty, the loader synthesizes one anchor person from this string.
subject: Pat Smith (Gen 1)
_comment_person_model: How person records are stored on disk. 'file' (default) = one Markdown file per person (the upstream/legacy model). 'narrative' = many people per lineage file, each a bold-name entry with an inline '- meta:' block. Both hold the same fields; omit this key to accept 'file'.
_comment_anchor: The vault's Generation 1. kind 'individual' = one person; kind 'couple' = the married couple (two people, both Gen 1). Converting individual to couple adds a second person's ancestry as new Gen-2+ entries and never renumbers existing entries.
_comment_repositories: Where the toolkit reads from and writes back to. Read and write auth are separate. visibility drives the living-person gate: a 'public' target skips living/unknown people; a 'private' target may include them. A write target ships enabled:false unless you opt in, so e.g. WikiTree stays corroboration-only by default.

wt._comment: Schema-capable but write-disabled by default: WikiTree stays read/corroboration-only until you set write.enabled true.

anc._comment: A PRIVATE personal tree can legally hold living people a public shared tree cannot, hence visibility 'private'.

_comment_hosts: Where a source record can be hosted. locator_kind 'ark' when the host mints ARKs (self-describing via the NAAN prefix), else 'url' or 'id'. Seeded with the hosts harvest_sources.py recognizes.
_comment_structural_gap: Entries that can essentially never acquire an indexed ARK (deep pre-registration lines). deep_gen_threshold: Gen >= this is peerage/visitation-documented. rules: region- or PID-scoped exemptions from the SOURCE_GAP to-do count.

rules.label: example: pre-1866 parish-resident line, registers not digitized online

known_dup_fs_pids.note: advisory baseline for the DUP_FS_PID display
